SeasonResult: St. Andrew's won its second straight SCISA Class A/AA boys soccer championship with a 5-1 win over James Island Christian at Heathwood Hall.
StatusNote: Archive build in progress. The season currently includes 12 known games, including an inferred May 8 region championship win over Beaufort Academy with no reported score. The adjusted final record is 13-1-1, matching the published state championship record line. The May 13 semifinal recap indicated at least one additional unreported win in the recovered schedule.
HistoricalSummary: The Spring 2004 boys soccer archive currently begins with St. Andrew's 4-1 region win over Cathedral Academy on March 22, 2004, followed by wins over Charleston Collegiate, Beaufort Academy, and James Island Christian, a 3-3 tie against Hilton Head Christian, an 8-0 win over Patrick Henry, region tournament wins over Cathedral Academy and Patrick Henry Academy, an inferred region championship win over Beaufort Academy, and state tournament wins over St. Francis Xavier, Cathedral Academy, and James Island Christian.

Hamish Huntley scored twice in the March 22 Cathedral Academy win, the March 23 Charleston Collegiate win, the April 6 Hilton Head Christian tie, the April 29 Patrick Henry win, and the May 4 Cathedral Academy win, then scored five goals against Patrick Henry Academy on May 6.

The May 8 region championship result was not reported, but the May 10 state tournament brief listed St. Andrew's at 10-1-1 after a 9-0 quarterfinal win over St. Francis Xavier. That record line indicates St. Andrew's won the May 8 region title match.
The May 12 Savannah Morning News recap listed St. Andrew's at 12-1-1 after a 7-1 state semifinal win over Cathedral Academy, sending the Saints to the SCISA Class AA/A championship match.
St. Andrew's defeated James Island Christian 5-1 on May 15 at Heathwood Hall to win its second straight SCISA Class A/AA boys soccer championship and finish 13-1-1.
HighlightNotes: The adjusted final record is 13-1-1 overall, 5-1 region, 2-0-1 non-region, and 6-0 in recovered tournament games.
St. Andrew's won the SCISA Region 2-AA tournament championship.
St. Andrew's won its second straight SCISA Class A/AA championship.
Hamish Huntley scored 19 goals across the recovered games.
Daniel Eichholz scored twice and had two assists in the state final.
Jacob Rauers scored in the state semifinal and state championship matches.
